# pointblank

> Find out if your data is what you think it is.

## Docs

### API Reference

#### Validate
> When performing data validation, use the `Validate` class to get the process started. It takes the target table and options for metadata and failure thresholds (using the `Thresholds` class or shorthands). The `Validate` class has numerous methods for defining validation steps and for obtaining post-interrogation metrics and data.


- [Validate](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.html): Workflow for defining a set of validations on a table and interrogating for results
- [Thresholds](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Thresholds.html): Definition of threshold values
- [Actions](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Actions.html): Definition of action values
- [FinalActions](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/FinalActions.html): Define actions to be taken after validation is complete
- [Schema](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Schema.html): Definition of a schema object
- [DraftValidation](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/DraftValidation.html): Draft a validation plan for a given table using an LLM
- [EditValidation](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/EditValidation.html): Edit an existing validation plan with a plain-English instruction using an LLM
- [MissingSpec](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/MissingSpec.html): Specification for structured missing values in a column

#### Contracts and Pipelines
> Use `Contract` and `Step` to define declarative data quality contracts that specify what valid data looks like. Use `Pipeline` to enforce contracts at both boundaries of a data transformation (source and target), producing a `PipelineResult` with full introspection into what passed and what failed.


- [Contract](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Contract.html): A declarative boundary contract for pipeline data
- [Step](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Step.html): A single validation step in a Contract, defined declaratively
- [Pipeline](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Pipeline.html): Binds source and target contracts into a pipeline boundary enforcement unit
- [PipelineResult](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/PipelineResult.html): Result of a pipeline boundary validation run

#### Contract Import/Export
> Import external schema definitions (JSON Schema, Frictionless Table Schema, and more) into Pointblank validation workflows, or export Pointblank contracts to those formats. Use `import_contract()` as the entry point, `export_contract()` for the reverse, and `register_adapter()` to add support for custom formats.


- [import_contract](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/import_contract.html): Import a contract/schema from an external format
- [export_contract](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/export_contract.html): Export a Pointblank validation or contract to an external format
- [list_adapters](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/list_adapters.html): List all registered adapters with their capabilities
- [register_adapter](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/register_adapter.html): Register a contract adapter class
- [ContractImport](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ContractImport.html): Result of importing an external contract/schema
- [ContractAdapter](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ContractAdapter.html): Base class for contract import/export adapters

#### Validation Steps
> Validation steps are sequential validations on the target data. Call `Validate`'s validation methods to build up a validation plan: a collection of steps that provides good validation coverage.


- [Validate.col_vals_gt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_gt.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_lt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_lt.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_ge](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_ge.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_le](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_le.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_eq](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_eq.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_ne](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_ne.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_between](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_between.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_outside](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_outside.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_in_set](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_in_set.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_not_in_set](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_not_in_set.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_increasing](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_increasing.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_decreasing](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_decreasing.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_null](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_null.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_not_null](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_not_null.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_regex](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_regex.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_within_spec](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_within_spec.html)
- [Validate.col_vals_expr](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_vals_expr.html)
- [Validate.col_exists](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_exists.html)
- [Validate.col_pct_null](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_pct_null.html)
- [Validate.col_pct_missing](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_pct_missing.html)
- [Validate.col_missing_coded](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_missing_coded.html)
- [Validate.col_missing_only_coded](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_missing_only_coded.html)
- [Validate.col_missing_consistent](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_missing_consistent.html)
- [Validate.rows_distinct](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.rows_distinct.html)
- [Validate.rows_complete](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.rows_complete.html)
- [Validate.col_schema_match](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_schema_match.html)
- [Validate.row_count_match](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.row_count_match.html)
- [Validate.col_count_match](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_count_match.html)
- [Validate.data_freshness](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.data_freshness.html)
- [Validate.tbl_match](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.tbl_match.html)
- [Validate.conjointly](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.conjointly.html)
- [Validate.specially](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.specially.html)
- [Validate.prompt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.prompt.html)

#### Aggregation Steps
> These validation methods check aggregated column values (sums, averages, standard deviations) against fixed values or column references.


- [Validate.col_sum_gt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sum_gt.html)
- [Validate.col_sum_lt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sum_lt.html)
- [Validate.col_sum_ge](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sum_ge.html)
- [Validate.col_sum_le](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sum_le.html)
- [Validate.col_sum_eq](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sum_eq.html)
- [Validate.col_avg_gt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_avg_gt.html)
- [Validate.col_avg_lt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_avg_lt.html)
- [Validate.col_avg_ge](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_avg_ge.html)
- [Validate.col_avg_le](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_avg_le.html)
- [Validate.col_avg_eq](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_avg_eq.html)
- [Validate.col_sd_gt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sd_gt.html)
- [Validate.col_sd_lt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sd_lt.html)
- [Validate.col_sd_ge](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sd_ge.html)
- [Validate.col_sd_le](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sd_le.html)
- [Validate.col_sd_eq](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.col_sd_eq.html)

#### Column Selection
> Use the `col()` function along with column selection helpers to flexibly select columns for validation. Combine `col()` with `starts_with()`, `matches()`, etc. for selecting multiple target columns.


- [col](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/col.html): Helper function for referencing a column in the input table
- [starts_with](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/starts_with.html): Select columns that start with specified text
- [ends_with](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ends_with.html): Select columns that end with specified text
- [contains](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/contains.html): Select columns that contain specified text
- [matches](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/matches.html): Select columns that match a specified regular expression pattern
- [everything](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/everything.html): Select all columns
- [first_n](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/first_n.html): Select the first `n` columns in the column list
- [last_n](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/last_n.html): Select the last `n` columns in the column list
- [expr_col](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/expr_col.html): Create a column expression for use in `conjointly()` validation

#### Segment Groups
> Combine multiple values into a single segment using `seg_*()` helper functions.


- [seg_group](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/seg_group.html): Group together values for segmentation

#### Interrogation and Reporting
> The validation plan is executed when `interrogate()` is called. After interrogation, view validation reports, extract metrics, or split data based on results.


- [Validate.interrogate](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.interrogate.html)
- [Validate.set_tbl](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.set_tbl.html)
- [Validate.get_tabular_report](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_tabular_report.html)
- [Validate.get_step_report](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_step_report.html)
- [Validate.get_json_report](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_json_report.html)
- [Validate.get_dataframe_report](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_dataframe_report.html)
- [Validate.to_code](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.to_code.html)
- [Validate.to_yaml](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.to_yaml.html)
- [Validate.suggest_improvements](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.suggest_improvements.html)
- [Validate.from_prompt](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.from_prompt.html)
- [Validate.get_sundered_data](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_sundered_data.html)
- [Validate.get_data_extracts](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_data_extracts.html)
- [Validate.all_passed](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.all_passed.html)
- [Validate.assert_passing](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.assert_passing.html)
- [Validate.assert_below_threshold](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.assert_below_threshold.html)
- [Validate.assert_dimension_scores](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.assert_dimension_scores.html)
- [Validate.above_threshold](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.above_threshold.html)
- [Validate.get_dimension_scores](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_dimension_scores.html)
- [Validate.get_health_score](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_health_score.html)
- [Validate.get_scorecard](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.get_scorecard.html)
- [Validate.n](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.n.html)
- [Validate.n_passed](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.n_passed.html)
- [Validate.n_failed](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.n_failed.html)
- [Validate.f_passed](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.f_passed.html)
- [Validate.f_failed](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.f_failed.html)
- [Validate.warning](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.warning.html)
- [Validate.error](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.error.html)
- [Validate.critical](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Validate.critical.html)

#### Inspection and Assistance
> Functions for getting to grips with a new data table. Use `DataScan` for a quick overview, `preview()` for first/last rows, `col_summary_tbl()` for column summaries, and `missing_vals_tbl()` for missing value analysis.


- [DataScan](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/DataScan.html): Get a summary of a dataset
- [preview](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/preview.html): Display a table preview that shows some rows from the top, some from the bottom
- [col_summary_tbl](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/col_summary_tbl.html): Generate a column-level summary table of a dataset
- [missing_vals_tbl](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/missing_vals_tbl.html): Display a table that shows the missing values in the input table
- [load_dataset](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/load_dataset.html): Load a dataset hosted in the library as specified table type
- [get_data_path](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_data_path.html): Get the file path to a dataset included with the Pointblank package
- [connect_to_table](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/connect_to_table.html): Connect to a database table using a connection string
- [print_database_tables](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/print_database_tables.html): List all tables in a database from a connection string

#### Table Pre-checks
> Helper functions for use with the `active=` parameter of validation methods. These inspect the target table before a step runs and conditionally skip the step when preconditions are not met.


- [has_columns](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/has_columns.html): Check whether one or more columns exist in a table
- [has_rows](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/has_rows.html): Check whether a table has a certain number of rows

#### YAML
> Functions for using YAML to orchestrate validation workflows.


- [yaml_interrogate](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/yaml_interrogate.html): Execute a YAML-based validation workflow
- [validate_yaml](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_yaml.html): Validate YAML configuration against the expected structure
- [yaml_to_python](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/yaml_to_python.html): Convert YAML validation configuration to equivalent Python code

#### Utility Functions
> Functions for accessing metadata about the target data and managing configuration.


- [get_column_count](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_column_count.html): Get the number of columns in a table
- [get_row_count](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_row_count.html): Get the number of rows in a table
- [get_action_metadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_action_metadata.html): Access step-level metadata when authoring custom actions
- [get_validation_summary](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_validation_summary.html): Access validation summary information when authoring final actions
- [write_file](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/write_file.html): Write a Validate object to disk as a serialized file
- [read_file](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/read_file.html): Read a Validate object from disk that was previously saved with `write_file()`
- [ref](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ref.html): Reference a column from the reference data for aggregate comparisons

#### Test Data Generation
> Generate synthetic test data based on schema definitions. Use `generate_dataset()` to create data from a Schema object, or `schema_from_tbl()` to infer a generation-ready schema from an existing table (Polars, Pandas, or Ibis/DuckDB).


- [generate_dataset](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/generate_dataset.html): Generate synthetic test data from a schema
- [schema_from_tbl](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/schema_from_tbl.html): Create a Schema from an existing table with inferred Field constraints
- [int_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/int_field.html): Create an integer column specification for use in a schema
- [float_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/float_field.html): Create a floating-point column specification for use in a schema
- [string_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/string_field.html): Create a string column specification for use in a schema
- [bool_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/bool_field.html): Create a boolean column specification for use in a schema
- [date_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/date_field.html): Create a date column specification for use in a schema
- [datetime_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/datetime_field.html): Create a datetime column specification for use in a schema
- [time_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/time_field.html): Create a time column specification for use in a schema
- [duration_field](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/duration_field.html): Create a duration column specification for use in a schema
- [profile_fields](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/profile_fields.html): Create a dict of string field specifications representing a person profile

#### Prebuilt Actions
> Prebuilt action functions for common notification patterns.


- [send_slack_notification](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/send_slack_notification.html): Create a Slack notification function using a webhook URL
- [emit_otel](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/emit_otel.html): Create an OTel export action for use in `FinalActions`

#### Metadata Import/Export
> Import variable-level metadata from external data standards files (CDISC Define-XML, Controlled Terminology, SPSS `.sav`, SAS XPORT, Stata `.dta`, and more) and export metadata to various formats. Use `import_metadata()` as the entry point and `export_metadata()` for the reverse.


- [import_metadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/import_metadata.html): Import metadata from an external standard or file
- [export_metadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/export_metadata.html): Export metadata to an external standard format
- [MetadataImport](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/MetadataImport.html): Parsed metadata from an external standard
- [MetadataPackage](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/MetadataPackage.html): A collection of `MetadataImport` objects from a multi-dataset source
- [VariableMetadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/VariableMetadata.html): Metadata for a single variable/column, as imported from an external standard
- [Codelist](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/Codelist.html): A controlled terminology / value set from an external standard
- [CodelistEntry](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/CodelistEntry.html): A single entry in a codelist (controlled terminology)
- [MissingValueCode](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/MissingValueCode.html): A structured missing value definition from an external standard

#### SDTM Validation
> Validate clinical datasets against CDISC SDTM domain templates. Use `validate_sdtm()` to generate a full `Validate` workflow, or `validate_sdtm_structure()` for a quick structural conformance check. Retrieve domain templates with `get_sdtm_domain()` and `list_sdtm_domains()`.


- [validate_sdtm](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_sdtm.html): Generate a comprehensive SDTM validation workflow for a dataset
- [validate_sdtm_structure](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_sdtm_structure.html): Validate the structural conformance of a dataset against an SDTM domain template
- [sdtm_to_metadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/sdtm_to_metadata.html): Convert an SDTM domain template to a `MetadataImport` object
- [get_sdtm_domain](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_sdtm_domain.html): Get the SDTM template for a specific domain
- [list_sdtm_domains](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/list_sdtm_domains.html): List all available SDTM domain codes
- [SDTMDomainTemplate](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/SDTMDomainTemplate.html): Structural template for an SDTM domain
- [SDTMVariableSpec](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/SDTMVariableSpec.html): Specification for a single variable in an SDTM domain template

#### ADaM Validation
> Validate analysis datasets against CDISC ADaM templates. Use `validate_adam()` to generate a full `Validate` workflow, or `validate_adam_structure()` for a quick structural conformance check. Retrieve dataset templates with `get_adam_dataset()` and `list_adam_datasets()`.


- [validate_adam](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_adam.html): Generate a comprehensive ADaM validation workflow for a dataset
- [validate_adam_structure](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_adam_structure.html): Validate structural conformance of a dataset against an ADaM template
- [adam_to_metadata](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/adam_to_metadata.html): Convert an ADaM dataset template to a MetadataImport object
- [get_adam_dataset](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/get_adam_dataset.html): Get the ADaM template for a specific dataset
- [list_adam_datasets](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/list_adam_datasets.html): List all available ADaM dataset template names
- [ADaMDatasetTemplate](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ADaMDatasetTemplate.html): Structural template for an ADaM dataset
- [ADaMVariableSpec](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ADaMVariableSpec.html): Specification for a single variable in an ADaM dataset template

#### CDISC Submission Conformance
> Validate SDTM datasets for CDISC conformance. `validate_sdtmig()` is the primary entry point: pass a dictionary of domain DataFrames and receive a `ConformanceReport` with 426 SDTMIG 3.4 rules evaluated in-process. For full submission-package checks (cross-dataset referential integrity, SUPP-- linkage, Define-XML) use `SubmissionPackage`. For the authoritative CDISC-certified rule set, use `validate_cdisc_submission()` (requires the CORE CLI) or `SubmissionPackage.validate_conformance(engine="core")`. All paths return a `ConformanceReport`.


- [validate_sdtmig](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_sdtmig.html): Validate SDTM datasets against the SDTMIG rule catalog and return a conformance report
- [validate_cdisc_submission](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/validate_cdisc_submission.html): Validate a CDISC submission with the CDISC CORE engine, in one call
- [SubmissionPackage](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/SubmissionPackage.html): A data-level model of a study submission package for CDISC conformance validation
- [ConformanceReport](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/ConformanceReport.html): The result of a CDISC conformance validation run

#### Integrations
> Classes for integrating Pointblank with external observability and monitoring systems. Use `OTelExporter` to export validation results as OpenTelemetry metrics, traces, and logs.


- [integrations.otel.OTelExporter](https://posit-dev.github.io/pointblank/reference/integrations.otel.OTelExporter.html)
